title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The UK government has responded to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, outlining its plans to address the recommendations made by a committee in 2020. The response aims to ensure the safe and beneficial use of AI in various sectors by setting public standards and regulations.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of using copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It likely examines the rights of copyright holders in relation to AI generated works and potential infringement issues.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This policy document outlines the Department for Education's stance on using generative AI in education. It likely discusses the potential benefits and limitations of incorporating generative AI into the education system.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan outlines a way for the Department for Transport (DfT) to collaborate with the transport sector to benefit from AI technology while controlling potential risks. This plan aims to make the most of AI's opportunities in transport while ensuring safety and responsible use.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This AI policy document is a progress report on the use of existing works protected by copyright in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It focuses on implementing Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government document outlines their response to guidelines on regulating artificial intelligence (AI) in medical devices, proposed by an advisory council. The document likely addresses how the government will implement or modify regulations for AI in medical applications based on the council's recommendations.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating artificial intelligence in qualifications. It responds to government requests to address the use of AI in the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government is responding to a House of Lords report on Artificial Intelligence, taking it seriously and not being complacent about its impact. The government is addressing the concerns outlined in the report.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing artificial intelligence and its relation to public standards. The review aims to provide guidance on the role of AI in the public sector.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Centre for Data Ethics and Innovation's submission focuses on the governance of artificial intelligence in the UK. The submission aims to contribute to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on this topic.
